sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Abnormal lab findings, including a positive Lyme titer.
History of Present Illness
Michelle is a 31-year-old female with a past medical history of type 2 diabetes and hypertension who presents with abnormal lab findings. She reports having had an insect bite approximately two weeks ago, which resulted in a positive Lyme titer. She describes feeling tired and lacking energy, and reports swollen, painful joints in her elbows. She has also experienced nausea and a decreased appetite, making it difficult to eat. Her blood sugars have been occasionally high and low, but generally within range. She is currently taking metformin and lisinopril. She denies high fevers and reports a rash resembling a bull's-eye on her abdomen.
Findings
Physical examination reveals swelling of the elbow joints bilaterally, with tenderness to palpation. A bull's-eye rash is noted on the left abdomen, which is warm to the touch but not painful. Vital signs are stable: blood pressure 132/82 mmHg, heart rate 78 bpm, temperature 98.6°F. The remainder of the physical exam is unremarkable.
Assessment
The patient's presentation is most consistent with Lyme disease, complicated by underlying type 2 diabetes and hypertension.
Plan

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value: I am ordering a doxycycline prescription for three weeks. I will also consult with an infectious disease specialist to discuss further management. A hemoglobin A1c will be ordered to monitor her diabetes. I will continue her current lisinopril dosage. I have scheduled a follow-up appointment for one week to assess her response to treatment. The nurse will check her vital signs and administer the medication.
